description: Splits the per-process heap into a small young block (~64KB) and a larger old block. Minor GC (gc_minor) copies only the young generation using the existing Cheney machinery with a narrower from_ranges — no write barriers needed because fz is purely immutable (old→young pointers are structurally impossible). Major GC (gc_major) extends the existing gc() to cover both blocks. Back-edge yield, FZ_SHOULD_YIELD, mid_flight_roots slab, and mailbox-as-root are unchanged — only the block layout and GC dispatch change. Theoretical basis: Baker's 'Cheney on the M.T.A.' Blocked by fz-02r (mid-flight GC scaffolding must exist first).
title: gen-gc: generational GC — young/old split with write-barrier-free minor collection
